_comment: Curated TypeScript / JavaScript package renames and removed packages. Each entry: old import name -> new canonical name. Add entries with care — these trigger lint warnings in real code.

request.reason: The 'request' package was officially deprecated in 2020. For Node 18+, use the built-in fetch. For broader compatibility, use undici or node-fetch.

mkdirp.reason: Node 10.12+ supports fs.mkdir with the recursive option natively. The mkdirp package is no longer needed for new code.

rimraf.reason: Node 14.14+ supports fs.rm with recursive and force options. rimraf is still maintained for cross-platform safety, but native works for most cases.

nock.reason: nock has been in maintenance-only mode. msw or undici's built-in MockAgent are the modern alternatives.

moment.reason: moment is in maintenance-only mode. It is large (~290 KB minified) and mutable. date-fns and luxon are tree-shakeable, immutable alternatives. Day.js is a 2 KB moment-compatible API.

glob.reason: The 'glob' package is the classic, but fast-glob and tinyglobby are faster and actively maintained for build tooling use cases.

tslib.reason: TypeScript 2.7+ inlines tslib helpers (e.g. __awaiter, __rest) automatically. The tslib npm package is only needed when targeting older runtimes.

node-uuid.reason: node-uuid was renamed to uuid in 2017. The uuid package is the maintained successor.

babel-polyfill.reason: babel-polyfill was deprecated in favor of core-js/stable with @babel/preset-env's useBuiltIns: 'usage' option.

q.reason: The 'q' promise library was the standard before native Promises landed. For new code, use native Promises; for throttling, use p-limit or p-queue.

axios-retry.reason: axios-retry is still maintained but for most retry patterns, a small axios interceptor is sufficient. Native fetch + undici also handles retries.
